back_translation: Theophilus, in my first book I wrote about all the things that Jesus began to do and began to teach,
until the day He was taken up, after He had given commands to the apostles whom He had chosen through the Holy Spirit.
After He had suffered, He showed Himself to them as being alive by many confirming proofs, appearing to them throughout forty days, and speaking about matters concerning the kingdom of God.
While He was gathered together with them, He commanded them, 'Do not leave Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father which you heard from Me.'
'For John gave baptism with water, but you will receive baptism with the Holy Spirit a few days from now.'
When they had gathered together, they asked Him, 'Lord, will You at this time restore the kingdom to Israel?'
He said to them, 'It is not your business to know the appointed times or various fixed periods, which the Father has set by His own authority,
but you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes down upon you, and you will be My witnesses in Jerusalem, throughout all Judea and Samaria, to the uttermost ends of the earth.'
When He had said these words, while they were gazing on, He was taken up, and a cloud received Him beyond their sight.
While they were gazing at the sky watching Him go, behold, two men wearing white garments came and stood beside them.
The two said, 'Men of Galilee, why do you stand staring at the sky? This Jesus who was taken up from you into heaven will come back in the same manner as you have seen Him go up into heaven.'
Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olive, which is near Jerusalem, at a distance that can be walked on the Sabbath day.

When they had entered the city, they went up to the upper room which was their lodging place: Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas son of James.
All these people together with one accord devoted themselves to prayer, along with the women, Mary the mother of Jesus, and with His younger brothers.
In those days, Peter stood up in the midst of the brothers (which was a crowd gathered of about one hundred and twenty people) and said,
'Brothers, the Scripture passage which the Holy Spirit foretold through the mouth of David concerning Judas, who was a guide for those who came to arrest Jesus, had to be fulfilled,
for he had been counted among us and received a share in this ministry.'
(This one took the wages of his wickedness and went and bought a piece of land; then he fell headlong, and his body burst open in the middle, and all his intestines spilled out.
This matter became known among all who dwelt in Jerusalem, so that that piece of land was called in their own language 'Akeldama,' which means 'Field of Blood.')
'For it is written in the book of Psalms, "May his dwelling be desolate, and let there be no one living in it," and, "Let another take his position of oversight."'
'Therefore, from among the men who have been with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
from the baptism of John until the day He was taken up from us, one of them must become a witness with us of His resurrection.'
Then they proposed two men: Joseph who was called Barsabbas, who had the nickname Justus, and Matthias.
Then they prayed, 'Lord, You who know the hearts of all people, please show which of these two You have chosen,
to take up the position of this ministry and apostleship, which Judas abandoned to go to his own place.'
Then they cast lots for the two of them; the lot fell on Matthias, and he was counted in with the eleven apostles.
